sous_titres.text: - He loves that old camera. - Come on, Yang.
- Don't forget to join us. - I will.
What are you doing? Come on.
[Jake] Yellow Equinox. Is that a new blend?
I'm sure I can make you something close to it. You know what kind of tea leaves?
Oh, no. No, no. It's not this kind of tea.
I'm looking for your crystallized teas.
Ah. Sorry, I-I don't carry tea crystals.
How can you be a tea shop and not carry tea crystals?
Why don't you let me make you something that might be close?
No. No, no, no. This is... I have to go.
And, you know, I've got a busy few weeks coming up, so...
- I know. Yang will be here. - So will Daddy.
Don't be too hard on him.
He told me there was a rush of customers towards the end of the day.
That's good news for Daddy. He needed that.
Did you like the gochujang sauce?
So you put the ingredients in the bowl and you mixed them all together?
Next time, I'm gonna Juliet the carrots.
She keeps asking why we're always busy.
- And Yang is... - No, she doesn't.
Um... Um, I'll make more time.
You know I'm not putting this on you, right?
I just want us to be a team.
[Host, over speaker] Welcome, families of four, to our monthly dance-off.
- Mm-hmm. - That's the furthest we've ever gone.
[Jake] It looks like we have three years left on the warranty,
- but they're not answering, so... - [Kyra] Can't you just go there?
[Jake] Yeah, I'm going to, but I don't know if the store is even there anymore.
I did a search. I can't find them anywhere.
We didn't buy from them directly, so...
No, the warranty is with Second Siblings.
I told you we should've just bought a new one.
He was new. I mean, practically.
And he'd only been in use for five days. Five days.
- And we saved... - But now he...
Never mind. Let's just get him fixed.
You need to get Mika to school.
Mika, come on, we need to go!
He shut down last night. He won't restart.
- You got a technician? - He's still under warranty.
I wouldn't take him back to Brothers & Sisters.
They're just going to try to get you to recycle him for a new model.
I have to go and get Mika.
Did she see Yang all dead-eyed?
- I bet it freaked her out. - Yeah.
Listen, I got a technician in Woodbury.
It's worth the trek. I'm going to send you his info.
No, I'll just... I'll take him to Quick Fix if I can't figure it out.
That place is for morons. Don't take him there.
My guy will fix him for a third of the cost. You tell Russ I sent ya.
Okay. Okay, got to go. Thank you.
Come on, kiddo, we gotta boogie.
I don't want to go to school. I want to be with Yang.
Mika, the longer we wait, the worse it's gonna be for Yang.
FYI, Mika's going to be with me for the day.
Well, you know, she just refused and I wasn't gonna drag her inside.
Mom, I can't. I want to be with Yang.
It's okay, darling. I just wanted to hear your voice.
You need to listen to Dad and be good and calm, okay?
- Is Yang going to be better? - Yes, he's gonna be...
[Kyra] Well, we don't know for sure, but...
We can't make any promises, but, Mika, listen,
if you can be good and calm for the rest of the day,
You just promised her a surprise.
- What is it, Mom? - That's different.
- Mom, what is it? - I wasn't trying to...
I appreciate you taking the day off.
Honey, be good and listen to Dad.
Oh, I need to go. I have to get ready for my presentation.
What is she gonna get me, Dad?
- I have no idea. - I think I know.
Just don't get your hopes up for anything specific.
[whispers] Gege, I know what she's gonna get me.
- Excuse me? - Can I help you?
Yeah, I was wondering if you could... I purchased a...
This used to be a store where you could buy, uh,
an older sibling for Chinese adoptions.
Yeah, I can see, but do you know what happened to that shop?
Before my store, they sold memory.
Memory-balia. Is that how you say it, memory-balia?
No, I'm not sure what it was before that.
Mika. Mika, come on, let's go.
Uh, it's a black box issue, okay?
Uh, so by law, we're not allowed to tamper
with the, uh, core interior, unfortunately.
So what is it that you do here?
Uh, well, we are certified with Brothers & Sisters, Incorporated,
to repair or replace up to 12 parts most commonly in need of service.
If you want to take a gander there at the 12-point diagnostic.
Yeah, but your diagnostics is just...
Um, well, we hooked up Yang to our certified analyzer,
and, uh, all 12 parts were in working order.
that there was a core malfunction, unfortunately, so...
So what? So there's nothing you can do?
Oh, there's a couple things we can do here.
Um, we can have him recycled at Brothers & Sisters.
Uh, if you do so before he begins to decompose,
you'll get $1,000 off on a new model, which is great.
If you don't want to have him recycled, which I completely understand,
we are equipped to turn his head
and his voice box into a virtual assistant,
and we'd salvage the remaining parts for ya.
But we would have to send the core to, uh, Brothers & Sisters.
No, no, no. That's not gonna happen. No, just give him back to me,
- and I'll get a second opinion. - Oh, sure. Of course.
Uh, so that'll be 250 for the diagnostics, please.
I said we were getting a second opinion.
It's already taken longer than I expected.
Yeah, it's a little... I'll explain later. We're almost there.
- All right. Keep me posted. - Okay.
[Russ] It is an interior core problem.
I need your permission to break open the core.
It's just... It's a bunch of corporate bullshit.
Do you know who made that a law?
They're just trying to protect their spyware.
they will have so much data on your family, it'll make your head spin.
- You promised to fix him. - Mika, I said wait outside.
You said he was gonna be fine.
Come on, man. You gotta make a decision.
And we'd all miss him terribly, but...
We bought Yang to connect Mika to her Chinese heritage, not to raise her.
Yeah, but we spent a lot of money on Yang. I mean, I'm not gonna...
you know, feel bad if he does more for Mika than teach her Chinese fun facts.
He does a lot more than that, but you're missing the point.
If we can't fix Yang, we're not gonna buy another sibling for Mika.
And we can't afford it anyway.
So we need to be more involved, more than we are now.
And not just in raising her,
in connecting Mika to her culture and heritage.
Like, that will be on us.
So we need to be ready and willing.
They were asking about my real parents.
I told them about Mom and Dad,
and they said, "No, your real parents."
They said Mom and Dad weren't really my parents. Not really.
I mean, they're our parents, but...
No. Something wonderful is happening here.
You see, this branch is from a different tree.
Yes, but now it's becoming an actual part of this tree.
Not really, though. It's just pretend.
Because it's just taped to the tree.
Oh, right. It looks like that, doesn't it?
Here, let's find one that's already attached.
This branch is also from another tree.
But look, you're connected to Mom and Dad,
You're a part of the family tree. For real.
Why do they move branches to different trees?
It's called grafting. They do it to make something new.
Some of the apples you love were produced by combining different trees.
It's an ancient technique that was used in China over 4,000 years ago.
But you should know that both trees are important.
Not just this one, but the one from this branch, too.
Your other family tree is also a vital part of who you are.
I want to go with Dad.
No, kiddo, you need to go to school today.
But Yang! I wanna see Yang!
Hey, what about your Pup-Pup? Don't you want to show your friends?
- Yang. - Yang's gonna be home soon.
[Kyra] But you need to go to school.
Yang would want that. Do it for gege.
He'd want me to show my Pup-Pup.
- [Kyra] Mm-hmm. - All right, I'm heading out.
- Okay. Let me know how it goes. - Will do.
[Russ] We've always known that some bots are equipped with spyware.
Especially the foreign ones. And now we have proof.
Now? So you've never seen these before.
What does it matter? It is what it is.
- Have you looked at the recordings? - No.
Can I see what's on here?
The connectors, they're nothing I've seen before.
[chuckles] This is some high-level shit, man.
Sorry, I'm... I'm just trying to wrap my head around it.
It's time to wake up, man.
So how do I buy a reader that I can see what's on this?
No one's selling a reader for that.
You'll have to have one specifically modified for those connectors.
Okay, I'm gonna give you a name.
There's no number, but this is where you find them.
How much do I owe you?
Like I said, I get paid to fix things.
I guess I'll take Yang back.
Why don't... Why don't you look what's on there first?
You might not want this bot in your house anymore.
You can take him, but also, he's gonna start decomposing.
You won't get as much value for him.
I can keep him hooked up on the simulator until you know more.
You're a friend of George, right?
[man on speaker] Sorry, she's in a meeting,
but I'd be happy to pass on a message.
Yeah, could you just tell her...
You know what? It's all right. I'll talk to her later.
Great. Should I have her call you back?
No. No, it's all right. Thank you.
[Jake] I was told I could talk to you.
I was looking for Max Walker, but they mentioned...
[woman] Are you here to talk some conspiracy shit?
- No. No, Russ told me that I could... - Russ?
Oh, man, you're one of them. I can't believe they let you back here.
I'm not one of them. I...
I was told Max might be able to help me with it.
- What is that? - Russ said it's spyware.
We bought him after we adopted our daughter. She's Chinese.
And my neighbor recommended Russ after Yang shut down, and Russ broke in...
I'm pretty sure it's a memory bank.
The memory banks allowed the technos to record a few seconds each day.
The labs were trying to understand what technos considered memorable.
the whole project was shut down immediately for violating privacy laws.
Oh, I'm not gonna lie. This...
If it's the regulator, then Russ is right.
I'm just being honest with you.
would be an invaluable contribution to this museum
and just to the whole understanding of techno-sapiens.
So I guess the real question's gonna be,
what do we pay you to have Yang and his memories in our collection?
[Jake] Could I have another one of these, please?
["Air on the G String" playing on speaker]
Because you're not here, and I have work to do.
- Is that what you're doing? - Yes.
So you just dropped her off over there?
No, his daughters invited her over to play.
You need to stop being so hard on George.
He likes you, and he's being really helpful.
I don't want to talk about this again.
And he paints his face for sporting events.
- What is wrong with you? - What's wrong with him?
Are you any closer to getting Yang fixed?
This is the third place you've taken him to.
Well, he's actually still at the repair shop.
I'm, uh, having a part examined. By a specialist.
So is he gonna be up and running again soon?
I'm not sure. You know, it's... it's complicated.
That's Mika. I got to go.
Yeah, there should be a certification card that came with Yang.
There's a serial number and a PIN on it.
Also, per our agreement, you'll need to bring Yang's body here tomorrow.
I wouldn't be surprised if Russ is trying to replace parts and steal the originals.
We will keep Yang's body preserved here in a bio-state
while you go and review the memories
- and decide what you want to do next. - Okay.
I've dedicated my life to understanding techno-sapiens.
- Do you like it? - [Kyra chuckles] I love it.
Brenda's meeting me at the office.
We're gonna stay overnight and work.
- Sorry I wasn't here earlier. - It's fine.
You'll need to take Mika to school and get her ready for the day.
- Do you think you can do that? - Yeah, of course.
All right. [sighs] I need to go.
So what did the part reveal?
You... We should know something soon.
[Jake] Try and get some sleep if you can.
[Yang] Walk to me, mei mei. Walk to me.
[Yang] They know you didn't mean it.
[whispering] Is it boring you to tears?
Do you get yourself water in the middle of the night?
Is that right? So you'd wake him up?
Not very loudly, because he can hear me even when I whisper.
I say, [whispers] "Gege, I want to get some water."
Then he joins me in the hallway, and we walk quietly to the kitchen.
Why don't you just ask him to get you a glass of water?
Then you can stay in bed.
I like getting my own water.
[Russ] What do you mean, you want him back?
- Did Max make the reader for you? - Max isn't there anymore.
- Where is he? - I don't know.
I can't believe they finally got him.
Please, just let me have Yang back.
- [tool rattles, clatters] - I want an explanation.
don't need to give you an explanation.
And since they're used for adoptions and language learning,
they haven't had to change much over the years.
No need to be faster or stronger.
Damn Russ just hacked his way into the core.
There's a proper way of opening it.
So if you don't mind, we're just gonna do a full check-up,
and we're gonna restore Yang as best we can.
It won't help with his off state,
but it should help with his preservation.
- He loves that old camera. - Come on, Yang.
- [Kyra] He loves that old camera. - [Jake] Come on, Yang.
[Yang] Just look here for one second.
[Jake] What are you doing? Come on.
[Jake] Do you mean the taste of tea?
Maybe. Is the taste why you like tea?
[Jake] It has something to do with it.
[Yang] Is it why you've given your life to tea?
[Jake] Given my life to tea, well, that sounds pretty serious.
I guess I've acquired a taste for tea.
But it's not why I became interested.
I think what I was really drawn to at the beginning was...
I think what I was really drawn to at the beginning was the idea of tea.
- The idea of tea. - The idea of tea.
Yeah, I saw this old documentary when I was in college.
And it was about this man
searching for the best tea in China.
It's about a man searching for the best tea in China.
Yes, I'm sure you have lots of interesting facts about tea in China.
I do. Would you like to hear some of them?
I'm sorry, you were saying about the man searching for tea.
Yeah, actually, I think that's it. I think it was his searching that compelled me.
You know, the pursuit of this illusive thing,
this process that was connected to the soil,
to the plants, to the weather, and to a way of life.
So that's what you like about tea?
The search, not so much the taste.
No, no, no, the taste, too.
There's this part in the film, it's a great part,
where the man is explaining to his German friend
why it's so difficult for him to describe the taste of tea.
He says, "There's no language for it.
There are no words to adequately express the mysterious nature of tea."
who's just standing right beside him with a cup of tea, says,
[in German accent] "Yes, but I imagine things
like you are walking through a forest,
and there are leaves on the ground,
and it just had rained, and the rain has stopped,
and it's damp, and you walk,
and somehow, that is all in this tea."
[in normal tone] I mean, I loved that so much.
"Somehow, that is all in this tea."
"Somehow, that is all in this tea."
God, I watched it over and over again.
God, I watched it over and over again.
I would like to watch this movie.
Well, maybe we can do it together.
- So do you believe it? - [Jake] What's that?
That a cup of tea can contain a world?
That you could taste a place, a time?
[Jake] Let's put it to the test, shall we?
So you want to smell it first.
You know, there's not just flavor and the aroma, there's history, too.
You know, traditionally, tea shops that were family businesses
passed down from one generation to an...
...generation to, you know this, from generation to generation.
Maybe it's time I started teaching you the trade.
Let's see if we can taste the world together.
I'm not sure if I can taste the forest.
Maybe I haven't the language for it.
I wish Chinese tea wasn't just about facts for me.
I like watching the way you make tea.
The way the leaves bloom and float and fall.
I wish I felt something deeper about tea.
I wish I had a real memory of tea in China.
Sorry. I lost my train of thought.
[Yang] I don't know if that's the question for me.
I don't know if that's the question for me.
Our senses are stored, never to be the same.
Be and be better, for they existed.
- Hey, what can I get for you? - Hey, do you know her?
Could you ask someone else? I think she used to work here.
I'm sorry, there's a line. I'm gonna need you to order something.
Oh, I'll have a double espresso.
And if I can speak to the manager, that would be great.
- But she worked here. - She doesn't work here anymore.
- But could you tell me her name? - Why do you want to find her?
And he's not doing so well right now. Health-wise.
And I thought she'd want to know.
- But you've never met her. - No.
And you don't know her name.
I was wondering if you'd ever seen her around the house.
I don't know, but Yang knew her.
I... I didn't know that bots, uh...
That techno beings could make friends on their own.
- They were just... - I-I don't know anything.
- I just want to find her. - Hmm.
Let me ask the girls. Maybe they know something.
Ladies, I need to see you.
Just get over here. And bring the twins.
Vicky just made the all-star softball team.
You gotta come watch. I mean, she's just great.
Mr. Fleming wants to know if you've seen her around.
- Can we go? - Yeah. Go ahead.
- I don't... - No. No, not at all.
You know, I think finding her, it might help.
- Yeah, I've seen her. - You have?
A couple times. At your place.
Did you ever talk to her?
Once. Yang asked me not to say anything.
- No, no, it's not... - Come on, Vic. No fastballs.
I just... I've never had the...
- It's... It's fine. - No, it's not.
Yeah, but she's really cool and nice. Like most of us.
Um, do you know her name?
It's Aidy or Ada, something like that.
- Look, I'm sorry if I've ever... - Can I go now?
I mean, she's just... [mimics ball whooshing]
Well, thanks, George. I really have to...
Uh, hey, maybe we can get a drink together, huh? Some time?
You miss him? Me, too. We just can't see him now.
Are we never gonna dance again?
But we might have to compete in the family of threes.
- I don't want to be a family of three! - Mika.
[Kyra] Daddy's trying his best. He's doing everything he can.
What about you, Mom? Do you want him back?
[Kyra] This is dragging on too long.
- [Jake] I just need a little more time. - We need to start focusing on Mika.
We're just making things more difficult for her.
If we can't get Yang fixed, we just... we need to move forward. Okay?
[Jake] I need to tell you something.
Try command "memory reveal info," and it should tell you if it's corrupt or not.
is it possible for cultural technos like Yang to...
Do you mean for humans to use them?
No, no, no, I mean, for Yang to be interested in...
There's a girl, and she showed up in his memories,
and, you know, we didn't know anything about her.
because I've never heard of cultural technos having that capacity.
I cannot tell you how much...
Oh, all right, okay. But we'll touch base soon?
We usually meet, and he wasn't answering my messages.
I know Yang loves being a part of this family.
Can I ask how you met?
He used to buy things all the time, which, you know,
was funny when I found out he was a techno.
But, yeah, we started talking, and...
eventually, he asked me if I wanted to do something.
Well, we never really talked about us in that way.
She's freaking out. You need to pick her up.
They said she punched a kid, and then she started screaming at everyone.
- Holy shit. - Are you able to get her now?
Yeah. Uh, yeah. I'm on my way.
- [Jake] Thanks again for this. - [Cleo] Oh, I understand.
I'm sure everyone misses him a lot.
He would have his own area.
We could put some of his memories on a loop,
or on a set of readers that visitors could wear.
Of course, any memory would have to be approved by you.
Do you want me to stop now? I can put some away.
[chuckles] I like them quite a bit.
of the favorite subjects of Chinese trade art in the mid-1800s?
No, I did not know that.
There's also a famous folk tale about two lovers
who die of broken hearts and reemerge as butterflies.
Also, the ancient Chinese philosopher Lao Tzu once said,
the rest of the world calls a butterfly."
That the end is also a beginning?
But do you believe it's true for all things?
I don't know. I'm not programmed to believe in that way.
humans are programmed to believe in such things, but...
I don't know if it's really in our best interest.
May I be honest with you?
Is not being honest an option for you?
I'm fine if there's nothing in the end.
Maybe I was programmed this way as well.
Does it ever make you feel sad?
Does it ever make you feel sad?
[Jake] Did Yang ever struggle with being a techno?
I mean, did he ever... want to be human?
It's just... That's such a human thing to ask, isn't it?
We always assume that other beings would want to be human.
What's so great about being human?
So he never struggled with it? He never... questioned it?
He did question if he was Chinese.
He'd ask that all the time.
Maybe he just wanted to help Mika understand what it means to be Chinese.
He was dedicated to his little sister.
Yeah, Yang was a good brother.
- Hello. - [Jake] Hi, could I speak to the owners?
We took him back a few days later, and they let us make an exchange.
Well, I can't remember the name.
- Maybe. Is that in Chinatown? - Yeah, it used to be.
I assume he was on when you bought him in a sleep state?
Would you like to compress beta or gamma?
There is not enough space. Would you like to compress gamma?
[Ada] Hi, we're here to help Emma Davidson.
[Yang] Your niece is going to like me.
Hey. Let's go get Yang. Come here. Go get him.
I wish he would have told me about her. Us.
I don't want Yang to be on display in the museum.
We should let them study his memories.
- Do you think so? - Yes, his existence mattered.
Hey, what are you doing up?
Don't want to say bye to gege.
♪ I want to be just like a melody ♪
